out_of_scope_findings: game/content/items/sports_baseball_card.json contains a single item that duplicates content already in sports_memorabilia_cards.json — should be consolidated
game/content/stores/sample_sports_store.json appears to be an early draft that predates sports_memorabilia.json — may need cleanup
game/content/customers/casual_browser.json is a generic fallback customer not tied to any store type — unclear if this is intentional design or placeholder

Legacy single-item JSON files (sports_baseball_card.json, games_retro_cartridge.json, video_rental_vhs.json, fakemon_booster.json, electronics_mp3_player.json) are superseded by the full content sets and should be removed
sample_sports_store.json appears to be an older version of sports_memorabilia.json with different schema (starting_budget vs starting_cash, missing fixtures) — should be consolidated

The sports store definition uses store_type 'sports' but item files use store_type 'sports' — consistent, but the DATA_MODEL.md example uses 'retro_games' format. Video rental items use 'rentals' which matches the store def
No customer type definitions exist yet for pocket_creatures or electronics stores — these should be created in the next planning cycle

game/content/customer/ (singular) contains casual_browser.json while game/content/customers/ (plural) contains all store-specific customer files — directory naming inconsistency that DataLoader will need to handle or one directory should be consolidated

Store definition daily_rent values don't match the daily_rent_per_size values in pricing_config.json (e.g., rentals store is 'medium' with daily_rent $60, but pricing_config says medium = $120). Need to decide which is authoritative — per-store override or size-based default.

game/content/customer/ (singular) contains casual_browser.json while all other customer files are in game/content/customers/ (plural) — this directory inconsistency should be resolved as part of issue-086
video_rental_vhs.json was missing from issue-086's cleanup list — now added
The frontier assessment's coverage gap counts are stale — they report 1-2 items per store when actual counts are 19-46

The casual_browser.json uses singular 'customer' directory path in some references but actually lives in 'customers/' — the issue-020 reference to 'game/content/customer/casual_browser.json' is a stale path

Medium-size stores (rentals, pocket_creatures, electronics) have daily_rent of $55-65 but pricing_config.json says daily_rent_per_size.medium is $120 — inconsistency between store definitions and economy config
Legacy sports_memorabilia.json in stores/ has the same broken starting_inventory as the pre-fix unified file — confirms it's a stale duplicate that should be removed

Constants.STARTING_CASH is 5000.0 but store_definitions.json has per-store starting_cash ranging from 500-800. Need to decide which is authoritative — probably the store definition, with Constants as a fallback default. Not covered by any issue.

EventBus is missing several signals referenced in SYSTEM_OVERVIEW.md: reputation_changed, money_changed, order_placed, order_delivered. These will need to be added incrementally as the corresponding systems are implemented, but there's no single issue tracking EventBus completeness.

casual_browser.json has a non-standard schema (spending_range instead of budget_range, missing store_types). Issue-086 notes this but doesn't fully resolve the schema mismatch. If kept, it needs a schema migration.

ProductDefinition resource (game/resources/product_definition.gd) exists but is not in RESOURCE_CLASS_SPEC.md and not referenced by any issue — may be legacy scaffold from initial repo setup. Should be evaluated for removal or formal inclusion.

game/autoload/event_bus.gd is missing inventory-specific signals that issues 005-007 will need (item_stocked, item_removed_from_shelf, item_added_to_inventory). These are documented in issue-005 but not yet added.
Constants.gd defines STARTING_CASH = 5000 but pricing_config.json and store_definitions.json use 500. This discrepancy will surface during issue-001/010 implementation.

The pricing_config.json uses daily_rent_per_size (small:50, medium:120, large:250) but store_definitions.json has per-store daily_rent values that don't match these size tiers (e.g., video_rental is medium but rent is $60, not $120). Need to reconcile which is authoritative — store-specific overrides or size-based defaults.

The casual_browser.json disposition is still unresolved (referenced in issues 020 and 086). It's a single-Dictionary format file that doesn't match any store. Decision needed: universal fallback customer type or delete as legacy.
Issue-036 (Progression system design) is the last remaining wave-2 design doc. It depends on issues 031 and 032, both now complete. Ready to be written next cycle.
